author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.

source.title: NPNF1-14. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of St. John and the Epistle to the Hebrews

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For God sent not His Son to condemn the world, but to save the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Many of the more careless sort of persons, using the lovingkindness of God to increase the magnitude of their sins and the excess of their disregard, speak in this way, “There is no hell, there is no future punishment, God forgives us all sins.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To stop whose mouths a wise man says, “Say not, His mercy is great, He will be pacified for the multitude of my sins; for mercy and wrath come from Him, and His indignation resteth upon sinners” ( Ecclus. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 6 ): and again, “As His mercy is great, so is His correction also.” ( Ecclus. xvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Where then,” saith one, “is His lovingkindness, if we shall receive for our sins according to our deserts?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That we shall indeed receive “according to our deserts,” hear both the Prophet and Paul declare; one says, “Thou shalt render to every man according to his work” ( Ps. lxii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 12 , LXX.); the other, “Who will render to every man according to his work.” ( Rom. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And yet we may see that even so the lovingkindness of God is great; in dividing our existence into two periods, the present life and that which is to come, and making the first to be an appointment of trial, the second a place of crowning, even in this He hath shown great lovingkindness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “How and in what way?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because when we had committed many and grievous sins, and had not ceased from youth to extreme old age to defile our souls with ten thousand evil deeds, for none of these sins did He demand from us a reckoning, but granted us remission of them by the washing of Regeneration, and freely gave us Righteousness and Sanctification.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “What then,” says one, “if a man who from his earliest age has been deemed worthy of the mysteries, after this commits ten thousand sins?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Such an one deserves a severer punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For we do not pay the same penalties for the same sins, if we do wrong after Initiation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this Paul declares, saying, “He that despised Moses’ law died without mercy under two or three witnesses; of how much sorer punishment, suppose ye, shall he be thought worthy, who hath trodden under foot the Son of God, and hath counted the blood of the Covenant an unholy thing, and hath done despite unto the Spirit of grace?” ( Heb. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Such an one then is worthy of severer punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet even for him God hath opened doors of repentance, and hath granted him many means for the washing away his transgressions, if he will.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Think then what proofs of lovingkindness these are; by Grace to remit sins, and not to punish him who after grace has sinned and deserves punishment, but to give him a season and appointed space for his clearing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For all these reasons Christ said to Nicodemus, “God sent not His Son to condemn the world, but to save the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For there are two Advents of Christ, that which has been, and that which is to be; and the two are not for the same purpose; the first came to pass not that He might search into our actions, but that He might remit; the object of the second will be not to remit, but to enquire.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore of the first He saith, “I came not to condemn the world, but to save the world” ( c. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 17 ); but of the second, “When the Son shall have come in the glory of His Father, He shall set the sheep on His right hand, and the goats on His left.” ( Matt. xxv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And they shall go, these into life; and these into eternal punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet His former coming was for judgment, according to the rule of justice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because before His coming there was a law of nature, and the prophets, and moreover a written Law, and doctrine, and ten thousand promises, and manifestations of signs, and chastisements, and vengeances, and many other things which might have set men right, and it followed that for all these things He would demand account; but, because He is merciful, He for a while pardons instead of making enquiry.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For had He done so, all would at once have been hurried to perdition.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For “all,” it saith, “have sinned, and come short of the glory of God.” ( Rom. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou the unspeakable excess of His lovingkindness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He that believeth on the Son, is not judged; but he that believeth not, is judged already.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet if He “came not to judge the world,” how is “he that believeth not judged already,” if the time of “judgment” has not yet arrived?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He either means this, that the very fact of disbelieving without repentance is a punishment, (for to be without the light, contains in itself a very severe punishment,) or he announces beforehand what shall be.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as the murderer, though he be not as yet condemned by the decision of the judge, is still condemned by the nature of the thing, so is it with the unbeliever.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since Adam also died on the day that he ate of the tree; for so ran the decree, “In the day that ye eat of the tree, ye shall die” ( Gen. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 17 , LXX.); yet he lived.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then “died” he?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By the decree; by the very nature of the thing; for he who has rendered himself liable to punishment, is under its penalty, and if for a while not actually so, yet he is by the sentence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Lest any one on hearing, “I came not to judge the world,” should imagine that he might sin unpunished, and should so become more careless, Christ stops such disregard by saying, “is judged already”; and because the “judgment” was future and not yet at hand, He brings near the dread of vengeance, and describes the punishment as already come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this is itself a mark of great lovingkindness, that He not only gives His Son, but even delays the time of judgment, that they who have sinned, and they who believe not, may have power to wash away their transgressions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He that believeth on the Son, is not judged.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He that “believeth,” not he that is over-curious: he that “believeth,” not the busybody.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what if his life be unclean, and his deeds evil?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is of such as these especially that Paul declares, that they are not true believers at all: “They profess that they know God, but in works they deny Him.” ( Tit. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But here Christ saith, that such an one is not “judged” in this one particular; for his works indeed he shall suffer a severer punishment, but having believed once, he is not chastised for unbelief.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how having commenced His discourse with fearful things, He has concluded it again with the very same? for at first He saith, “Except a man be born of water and of the Spirit, he cannot enter into the Kingdom of God”: and here again, “He that believeth not on the Son, is judged already.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Think not,” He saith, “that the delay advantageth at all the guilty, except he repent, for he that hath not believed, shall be in no better state than those who are already condemned and under punishment.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And this is the condemnation, that light is come into the world, and men loved darkness rather than light.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What He saith, is of this kind: “they are punished, because they would not leave the darkness, and hasten to the light.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And hence He goes on to deprive them of all excuse for the future: “Had I come,” saith He, “to punish and to exact account of their deeds, they might have been able to say, ‘this is why we started away from thee,’ but now I am come to free them from darkness, and to bring them to the light; who then could pity one who will not come from darkness unto light?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When they have no charge to bring against us, but have received ten thousand benefits, they start away from us.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this charge He hath brought in another place, where He saith, “They hated Me without a cause” ( John xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 25 ): and again, “If I had not come and spoken unto them, they had not had sin.” ( John xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he who in the absence of light sitteth in darkness, may perchance receive pardon; but one who after it is come abides by the darkness, produces against himself a certain proof of a perverse and contentious disposition.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Next, because His assertion would seem incredible to most, (for none would prefer “darkness to light,”) He adds the cause of such a feeling in them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What is that?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Because,” He saith, “their deeds were evil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For every one that doeth evil, hateth the light, neither cometh to the light, lest his deeds should be reproved.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet he came not to judge or to enquire, but to pardon and remit transgressions, and to grant salvation through faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then fled they?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Had He come and sat in His Judgment seat, what He said might have seemed reasonable; for he that is conscious to himself of evil deeds, is wont to fly his judge.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But, on the contrary, they who have transgressed even run to one who is pardoning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If therefore He came to pardon, those would naturally most hasten to Him who were conscious to themselves of many transgressions; and indeed this was the case with many, for even publicans and sinners sat at meat with Jesus.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then is this which He saith?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He saith this of those who choose always to remain in wickedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He indeed came, that He might forgive men’s former sins, and secure them against those to come; but since there are some so relaxed, so powerless for the toils of virtue, that they desire to abide by wickedness till their latest breath, and never cease from it, He speaks in this place reflecting upon these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For since,” He saith, “the profession of Christianity requires besides right doctrine a sound conversation also, they fear to come over to us, because they like not to show forth a righteous life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Him that lives in heathenism none would blame, because with gods such as he has, and with rites as foul and ridiculous as his gods, he shows forth actions that suit his doctrines; but those who belong to the True God, if they live a careless life, have all men to call them to account, and to accuse them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So greatly do even its enemies admire the truth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe, then, how exactly He layeth down what He saith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: His expression is, not “He that hath done evil cometh not to the light,” but “he that doeth it always, he that desireth always to roll himself in the mire of sin, he will not subject himself to My laws, but chooses to stay without, and to commit fornication without fear, and to do all other forbidden things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if he comes to Me, he becomes manifest as a thief in the light, and therefore he avoids My dominion.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For instance, even now one may hear many heathen say, “that they cannot come to our faith, because they cannot leave off drunkenness and fornication, and the like disorders.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Well,” says some one, “but are there no Christians that do evil, and heathens that live discreetly?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That there are Christians who do evil, I know; but whether there are heathens who live a righteous life, I do not yet know assuredly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For do not speak to me of those who by nature are good and orderly, (this is not virtue,) but tell me of the man who can endure the exceeding violence of his passions and (yet) be temperate.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if the promise of a Kingdom, and the threat of hell, and so much other provision, can scarcely keep men in virtue, they will hardly go after virtue who believe in none of these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Or, if any pretend to do so, they do it for show; and he who doth so for show, will not, when he may escape observation, refrain from indulging his evil desires.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: However, that we may not seem to any to be contentious, let us grant that there are right livers among the heathen; for neither doth this go against my argument, since I spoke of that which occurs in general, not of what happens rarely.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And observe how in another way He deprives them of all excuse, when He saith that, “the light came into the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Did they seek it themselves,” He saith, “did they toil, did they labor to find it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The light itself came to them, and not even so would they hasten to it.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if there be some Christians who live wickedly, I would argue that He doth not say this of those who have been Christians from the beginning, and who have inherited true religion from their forefathers, (although even these for the most part have been shaken from right doctrine by their evil life,) yet still I think that He doth not now speak concerning these, but concerning the heathen and the Jews who ought to have come to the right faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He showeth that no man living in error would choose to come to the truth unless he before had planned for himself a righteous life, and that none would remain in unbelief unless he had previously chosen always to be wicked.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Do not tell me that a man is temperate, and does not rob; these things by themselves are not virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For what advantageth it, if a man has these things, and yet is the slave of vainglory, and remains in his error, from fear of the company of his friends?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This is not right living.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The slave of a reputation is no less a sinner than the fornicator; nay, he worketh more and more grievous deeds than he.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But tell me of any one that is free from all passions and from all iniquity, and who remains among the heathen.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thou canst not do so; for even those among them who have boasted great things, and who have, as they say, mastered avarice or gluttony, have been, most of all men, the slaves of reputation, and this is the cause of all evils.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus it is that the Jews also have continued Jews; for which cause Christ rebuked them and said, “How can ye believe, which receive honor from men?” ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And why, pray, did He not speak on these matters with Nathanael, to whom He testified of the truth, nor extend His discourse to any length?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because even he came not with such zeal as did Nicodemus.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For Nicodemus made this his work, and the season which others used for rest he made a season for hearing; but Nathanael came at the instance of another.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet not even him did Jesus entirely pass by, for to him He saith, “Hereafter ye shall see heaven open, and the angels of God ascending and descending upon the Son of Man.” ( c. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But to Nicodemus He spake not so, but conversed with him on the Dispensation and on eternal life, addressing each differently and suitably to the condition of his will.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It was sufficient for Nathanael, because he knew the writings of the prophets, and was not so timid either, to hear only thus far; but because Nicodemus was as yet possessed by fear, Christ did not indeed clearly reveal to him the whole, but shook his mind so as to cast out fear by fear, declaring that he who did not believe was being judged, and that unbelief proceeded from an evil conscience.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since he made great account of honor from men, more than he did of the punishment; (“Many,” saith the Evangelist, “of the rulers believed on Him, but because of the Jews they did not confess”— c. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 42 ;) on this point Christ toucheth him, saying, “It cannot be that he who believeth not on Me disbelieveth for any other cause save that he liveth an unclean life.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Farther on He saith, “I am the Light” ( c. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 12 ), but here, “the Light came into the world”; for at the beginning He spoke somewhat darkly, but afterwards more clearly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet even so the man was kept back by regard for the opinion of the many, and therefore could not endure to speak boldly as he ought.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Fly we then vainglory, for this is a passion more tyrannical than any.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hence spring covetousness and love of wealth, hence hatred and wars and strifes; for he that desires more than he has, will never be able to stop, and he desires from no other cause, but only from his love of vainglory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For tell me, why do so many encircle themselves with multitudes of eunuchs, and herds of slaves, and much show?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not because they need it, but that they may make those who meet them witnesses of this unseasonable display.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If then we cut this off, we shall slay together with the head the other members also of wickedness, and there will be nothing to hinder us from dwelling on earth as though it were heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nor doth this vice merely thrust its captives into wickedness, but is even co-existent with their virtues, and when it is unable entirely to cast us out of these, it still causeth us much damage in the very exercise of them, forcing us to undergo the toil, and depriving us of the fruit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he that with an eye to this, fasts, and prays, and shows mercy, has his reward.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What can be more pitiable than a loss like this, that it should befall man to bewail himself uselessly and in vain, and to become an object of ridicule, and to lose the glory from above?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since he that aims at both cannot obtain both.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is indeed possible to obtain both, when we desire not both, but one only, that from heaven; but he cannot obtain both, who longs for both.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore if we wish to attain to glory, let us flee from human glory, and desire that only which cometh from God; so shall we obtain both the one and the other; which may we all enjoy, through the grace and loving kindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom and with whom, to the Father and the Holy Ghost, be glory for ever and ever.
